name: SQL injection and path traversal in admin search endpoint

planted_issues.description: User-supplied query_term from request.args is interpolated directly into an SQL query via f-string (line 36). An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL, exfiltrate data, or drop tables. The sort_field parameter is also unsanitized, enabling ORDER BY injection.

The export_path parameter is taken directly from user input and passed to open() without any validation or sandboxing (line 43). An attacker can write to arbitrary filesystem paths (e.g., /etc/crontab, ~/.ssh/authorized_keys), achieving remote code execution.

The error handler returns the full Python traceback to the client (line 56). This leaks internal implementation details, file paths, library versions, and potentially database schema information to attackers.

clean_areas.description: The require_admin decorator correctly validates the admin token via the auth service and returns 401 on failure. The decorator pattern using functools.wraps is properly implemented.
